div.p: William by the grace of God king of the Scots to Bishops, Earls, Barons, Justices, Sheriffs, Officials, and all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted and given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, the land that they have from Patrick of Ryedale in the territory of Whitton, by those bounds that are contained in the charter of Patrick of Ryedale which they have from him concerning the same land; so that they have and possess whatever is within the bounds for ever, as freely and quitely, honourably and peaceably, as any alms is freely and quitely and honourably possessed in my kingdom. For I have given to them and confirmed that land free from all aids, pleas, gelds, and from every service and custom and secular exaction; so that I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant it for them against all men for ever, saving my service from Patrick and his heirs. As witnesses, Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; David my brother; Walter Oliphant, justice; Alan son of Walter; Hugh, clerk of the seal; Hugh Ridel; Walter of Windsor; Walter of Berkeley, chamberlain; William de Mortimer; Robert of Berkeley; John of London. At Lanark.
